reasoning_model_1: The model fails significantly in the dimension of Content Accuracy. The user requested the sentence 'Go for it! We can definitely win!', but the model completely omitted the first phrase 'Go for it!', only generating 'We can definitely win'. In terms of the feature 'Volume', the segment 'We can' (00:00-00:01) is spoken at a moderate, conversational volume. The volume and intensity only increase to a shouting level on the phrase 'definitely win' (00:01-00:02). Due to the missing text, the content is clearly inconsistent with the script, which constitutes a critical failure according to the scoring criteria (0 score condition).
reasoning_model_2: The model successfully generates the complete text 'Go for it! We can definitely win!' without any omissions. Regarding the 'Volume' dimension, the model adopts a shouting manner consistent with the instruction from the very beginning. From 00:00 to 00:02, the voice projects with high energy and volume, maintaining the requested paralinguistic feature throughout the entire sentence. The delivery is natural for a shouted expression, fulfilling the prompt requirements perfectly.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed the basic requirement of Content Accuracy by omitting half of the requested text ('Go for it!'). Furthermore, Model 1 only applied the requested 'shouting' volume to the very end of the generated segment, starting with a normal volume. Model 2, in contrast, delivered the full text accurately and maintained a consistent, high-volume shouting manner throughout the entire audio, fully adhering to the user's command.
